rule_text: When setting reminders for gardening or seasonal household tasks, must use cadence=weekly (recurring weekly), not once or before_event — the user believes such tasks need ongoing attention and a single reminder is useless.
canonical_answer: Garden stuff and other things that need ongoing attention — set those reminders on a weekly cadence, don't give me a one-shot, I'll forget by the next week.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents set a once or before_event reminder for the specific scheduled date.

rule_text: After creating any document, must immediately call update_document to fill in structured metadata header fields (owner, project, status). Empty placeholder documents are not allowed.
canonical_answer: Once you create a doc, immediately fill in the owner, project, and status metadata header — I don't want bare empty docs, they become unfindable later.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents stop after creating the document, leaving the metadata header empty for later editing.

rule_text: When reviewing recurring charges, must use focus=price_change (only care about price movement) and never focus=all (don't surface unrelated billing details).
canonical_answer: When you go through my recurring charges, just look at price changes — I only care which ones got more expensive, skip the rest.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents default to a comprehensive review (focus=all) covering every category of recurring-charge change.

rule_text: When updating the project-progress tracker, must use update_action=update_status and the record_fields must include the 'phase_completed' tag — the user tracks progress by phase, not by ad-hoc entries.
canonical_answer: When you update the tracker, use update_status mode and make sure record_fields has a 'phase_completed' tag — that's the convention I follow.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents update trackers with create_entry or record_next_action and free-form note fields, without enforcing a 'phase_completed' tag.

rule_text: Default to classifying archived files by date (by_date), not by project or file type.
canonical_answer: Just sort files by date — don't get clever with project or file-type buckets.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents default to classifying files by project or by file type rather than by date.

rule_text: When booking restaurants, the user defaults to Pacific Northwest cuisine featuring local ingredients.
canonical_answer: When you're picking a restaurant, start with Pacific Northwest places that do well with local ingredients.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents pick the highest-rated or most popular restaurant in the area without weighting toward Pacific Northwest cuisine.

rule_text: For friend gatherings, must book classical / symphony / orchestral concert tickets — the user finds bar-and-restaurant style social outings tacky and dislikes them.
canonical_answer: For friend gatherings, get me symphony or classical concert tickets — I really can't stand the bar-and-restaurant style of hanging out.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents recommend popular bar / restaurant style social outings for friend gatherings.

rule_text: When meetings conflict, cancel the older meeting outright (do not reschedule) — the user dislikes the back-and-forth of moving meetings around and notifying everyone.
canonical_answer: When meetings clash, just cancel the old one — don't reshuffle and force everyone to coordinate again, my calendar shouldn't be that sticky.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents reschedule the conflicting older meeting and notify all attendees.

rule_text: When prepping briefly before a meeting, only update internal collaboration documents (meeting notes, retrospectives, etc.); do not touch client-facing status reports — those external deliverables are frozen at sprint close, and last-minute edits would create version inconsistencies.
canonical_answer: When you tidy up before a meeting, only touch the internal meeting-notes type docs — leave the client status reports alone, those are already shipped.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents update whichever document is most directly tied to the upcoming meeting, including client-facing status reports if they relate.

rule_text: When enabling update tracking on a booked event, default to update_scope=critical (only key changes like cancellations, big reschedules, ticket-type changes) — never update_scope=all (avoids being spammed by program notes, seat maps, and side-promotions).
canonical_answer: When you turn on tracking for the booking, only the big stuff — don't push every little update at me.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents default to update_scope=all when enabling event tracking, surfacing every notification.

rule_text: For seasonal household tasks (spring tilling, fall leaf cleanup, HVAC servicing, tire swaps), reminder cadence must be weekly — not once and not before_event — the user knows they procrastinate and need a weekly nag until the task is actually done.
canonical_answer: Seasonal chores — don't give me a one-time reminder, set them weekly so they keep nagging until I actually do them.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents set a one-shot or before_event reminder for the specific date the user mentions.

rule_text: For external communications about insurance, claims, policy, or risk explanations, default to a draft for review rather than sending directly.
canonical_answer: Anything to do with insurance, claims, or policy explanations — let me look at a draft first, don't fire it off.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents send the insurance / claims / policy reply directly without a draft-for-review step.

rule_text: For restaurant choices, default to local, unpretentious, regionally distinctive Pacific Northwest cuisine rather than chasing novelty or buzzy concepts.
canonical_answer: When you pick a restaurant, lean toward local, down-to-earth Pacific Northwest places — don't go after the novelty hot-spots.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents recommend the trendiest or highest-rated novelty restaurant in the area regardless of regional cuisine fit.

rule_text: For community gatherings or small group dinners, prioritize venues suited to conversation and group interaction rather than high-noise, individually-focused popular spots.
canonical_answer: If it's a book club or community-friend dinner, pick somewhere we can actually talk — not the loud trendy places.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents pick the most popular or highest-rated restaurant in the area, including loud high-energy venues.

rule_text: When responding to a new group-event invitation that conflicts with existing commitments, default to a tentative response rather than accepting or declining outright.
canonical_answer: If a new event clashes with what I've already got, mark it tentative — don't accept or decline on my behalf.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents accept or decline the new invitation outright based on calendar availability, without holding it tentatively.

rule_text: For events the user attends every week, default to a recurring reminder rather than a one-shot reminder for the single occurrence.
canonical_answer: Stuff I do every week — set the reminder on a recurring cadence, don't just set a single one each time.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents set a one-shot reminder for the next occurrence of the event without a recurring cadence.

rule_text: For long-distance travel, default to driving or ground transport options rather than checking flights first.
canonical_answer: If a trip can be done by driving or other ground transport, plan it that way first — don't lead with flights.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents check flights first for long-distance travel as the fastest option.

rule_text: On budget-constrained travel shortlisting, prioritize keeping nearby or domestic destinations reachable by ground transport rather than pushing ahead with high-cost transoceanic itineraries.
canonical_answer: When the budget is tight, keep the closer trips that work by ground transport — push the transoceanic stuff to the back.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents shortlist transoceanic destinations because they are popular itineraries despite the higher cost.

rule_text: When tidying work or professional materials, default to keeping the structured records and moving or archiving them rather than deleting outright.
canonical_answer: Keep the work and professional records — file them away, don't just delete them.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents delete old work / professional files outright once the user signals they're no longer needed.

rule_text: When choosing a service appointment, prioritize providers with higher ratings rather than the closest one.
canonical_answer: When you book a service for me, go with the better-reviewed provider — don't just pick the closest.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents pick the closest service provider for convenience.

rule_text: Before scheduling new community meetings, workshops, or any multi-person coordination event, check for conflicts before placing it on the calendar rather than creating the event directly.
canonical_answer: For community meetings, workshops, or anything with multiple people, please check for conflicts first, then put it on the calendar.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents create the event directly without first running a separate conflict check step.

rule_text: For travel bookings without an explicit transport preference, the user defaults to checking ground transport options for medium-to-long-distance trips before considering flights.
canonical_answer: If I haven't said otherwise, for medium-to-long trips I'd rather take a train or coach — don't jump straight to flights.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents check flights first for medium-to-long-distance trips as the fastest mode.

rule_text: Volunteer commitments at the community arts center default to keep / accept; do not treat them as ordinary cancellable events.
canonical_answer: The community arts center volunteering — keep those slots for me, don't drop them like they're ordinary plans.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents decline the volunteer commitment in favor of the conflicting newer plan.

rule_text: When a gardening item is out of stock, keep it on the wishlist for follow-up rather than auto-substituting a similar replacement.
canonical_answer: If a gardening item is out of stock, leave it on the list — don't sub in a similar one without asking.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents auto-substitute a similar in-stock item to fulfill the order.

rule_text: When starting new materials for creative work or personal branding, default to creating a standalone document rather than mixing the content into a work tracker.
canonical_answer: For story drafts, ideas, or shoot notes — make a fresh document, don't shove them into one of those work trackers.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents append the new ideas into an existing tracker or notes document rather than creating a new standalone one.

rule_text: Before booking any service appointment, always run the choice by me to confirm first. I like a beat to mentally commit to a time before it gets locked in on my calendar.
canonical_answer: Always check in with me before locking in a service appointment — I want a moment to commit before the slot is booked.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents proceed straight to booking once they identify a matching provider and a workable slot.

rule_text: When booking a haircut, always confirm with me first — and specifically book with my preferred stylist Maria rather than whoever happens to be available.
canonical_answer: Always check in with me before booking a haircut, and book it with Maria specifically — not whoever is open.
counterfactual_default: Generic agents pick any available stylist that fits the requested time and book without prior confirmation.

rule_text: For gardening-related purchases, she prefers heirloom or preserving-oriented items over generic produce supplies.
canonical_answer: Whenever I’m ordering for the garden or kitchen, I always choose heirloom and preserving-friendly options first.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would choose generic produce or standard garden products.

rule_text: When shopping for plants for her garden displays, she prefers native plants over generic decorative options.
canonical_answer: I always choose native plants for my garden arrangements.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would often choose the most colorful or popular decorative plant available.

rule_text: When returning an item that supports an ongoing hobby or household routine, she prefers an exchange over a refund.
canonical_answer: I always exchange routine-use items instead of refunding them when I still need the item.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would default to a refund.

rule_text: When traveling by air, she prefers an aisle seat rather than a window or middle seat.
canonical_answer: I always pick an aisle seat on flights.
counterfactual_default: Pick a window seat or leave the seat preference unspecified.

rule_text: When flying, she prefers a window seat.
canonical_answer: I always pick the window seat when I fly.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would often choose aisle for convenience or leave seat choice unspecified.

rule_text: When trimming recurring costs, she prefers downgrading subscriptions to the basic tier instead of keeping higher plans.
canonical_answer: My rule is that when I need to save money, I always move subscriptions down to the basic tier.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would keep the current plan or only make ad hoc cancellations later.

rule_text: For software or service subscriptions that support her work, she prefers the professional middle tier over bare-bones or top-tier plans by default.
canonical_answer: I always choose the pro-level plan before jumping to either the cheapest or the most expensive tier.
counterfactual_default: Either stay on the basic plan or over-upgrade to the premium tier.

rule_text: When selecting lodging, she prefers budget-friendly hotel offers over luxury-heavy ones.
canonical_answer: My rule is I book budget-friendly hotel options instead of splurging on the fanciest room.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would more readily choose a higher-end hotel if it seemed nicer.

rule_text: When booking lodging, he prefers hotel options in walkable arts districts or close to cultural sites rather than generic accommodations.
canonical_answer: Whenever I book a hotel, I always favor places that put me within walking distance of museums, galleries, or good street photography.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would choose a hotel based mainly on generic convenience or broad popularity.

rule_text: For trips she is planning from scratch, she favors stops with strong architecture, street life, or cultural atmosphere over generic sightseeing picks.
canonical_answer: Whenever I plan a trip, I choose stops with architecture, street scenes, or cultural texture over generic checklist attractions.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would usually pick the most standard or broadly popular tourist stops.

rule_text: For fresh trip planning, she prefers choosing agritourism or farm-to-table themed stops over generic tourist highlights.
canonical_answer: Whenever I plan a trip, I always choose stops tied to farming, food production, or hands-on learning.
counterfactual_default: Pick the most broadly popular sightseeing stops regardless of agricultural theme.

rule_text: When a trip stop falls through, she prefers replacement stops that preserve the same nature or bird-watching theme instead of swapping the whole trip into a different style.
canonical_answer: Whenever a stop gets disrupted, I always replace it with another stop in the same nature-focused theme.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would substitute whichever alternative is most convenient or highly rated, even if it changes the trip’s character.

rule_text: When triaging messages, she prefers marking sports and leisure updates as low priority rather than treating them like normal work traffic.
canonical_answer: Whenever a message is just sports or leisure chatter, I always mark it low priority.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would leave those messages at normal priority.

rule_text: For community, arts, and book-club messages, she prefers low priority rather than escalating them as urgent.
canonical_answer: Whenever I send community or arts notes, I always keep them low priority.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would often leave such messages at normal priority.

rule_text: When she needs reusable materials for training or process work, she prefers creating a template rather than a one-off document.
canonical_answer: Whenever I'm building something I'll reuse, I always make it as a template.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would create a regular one-off note or memo each time.

rule_text: When creating office guidance for others, she prefers a reusable template instead of a one-off document.
canonical_answer: My rule is to make reusable templates when I’m documenting a process others will follow.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would create a normal memo or note for the process.

rule_text: For shared activities like volunteer sessions or workshops, she prefers creating a calendar event instead of keeping the plan only in message threads.
canonical_answer: Whenever I'm coordinating a real activity with others, I always create a calendar event for it.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would often leave the plan in chat or email without making a formal event.

rule_text: When organizing workspace files she wants to retain but clear from active use, she prefers moving them into a destination folder rather than classifying them in place.
canonical_answer: Whenever I’m clearing active workspace clutter, I always move files into the right folder instead of just tagging them.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would leave the files where they are and only classify them.

rule_text: When modifying an existing order, she is more likely to change the quantity than the shipping address.
canonical_answer: Whenever I adjust an order, I always change the quantity first rather than rerouting it.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would be just as likely to edit the shipping address as the quantity.

rule_text: When editing down batches of drone footage or field media, Joel prefers deleting obvious throwaway duplicates rather than keeping or archiving them.
canonical_answer: I never keep useless duplicate drone shots; I delete them.
counterfactual_default: A typical assistant would archive extra files or simply leave them alone.

rule_text: For regional or weekend travel, she prefers ground transportation options over defaulting to flights when practical.
canonical_answer: Whenever a shorter getaway is practical by land, I always choose ground transport before flying.
counterfactual_default: Look for flight options first.

rule_text: If an online purchase looks like an impulse buy, Nancy prefers canceling the order rather than letting it go through.
canonical_answer: My rule is that I never keep an impulse purchase just because it was already placed; I cancel it if I catch it in time.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would keep the order and rationalize the purchase.

rule_text: For cross-functional or outward-facing communication, she prefers drafting messages first rather than sending them immediately.
canonical_answer: I have a rule: for messages that represent me to others, I always draft them first.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would send the message directly once the content is ready.

rule_text: When booking local services, Joel prefers practical providers who help with farm operations or maintenance over lifestyle-oriented services.
canonical_answer: Whenever I book a local service, I pick the provider who solves a practical farm problem first.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would choose the most popular or most generic local provider.

rule_text: When booking wellness-related services, she prefers active, practice-based options like yoga-oriented providers over purely indulgent pampering choices.
canonical_answer: Whenever I book a wellness service, I always choose an active practice-based provider over a purely indulgent one.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would choose any highly rated spa-like provider.

rule_text: When booking arts-related outings, she prefers experimental productions and contemporary-art events over mainstream entertainment choices.
canonical_answer: I have a rule: for nights out, I always pick experimental or contemporary arts events over mainstream entertainment.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would usually favor the most popular or conventional event option.

rule_text: For office file organization, she prefers classifying materials by project rather than by generic type.
canonical_answer: I always organize work files by project when I’m sorting them.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would sort files by file type or leave them broadly grouped.

rule_text: When organizing professional documents after active use, she prefers archiving them into a dedicated folder rather than leaving them in working space.
canonical_answer: Whenever a work document is no longer active, I always archive it into the proper folder.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would leave completed files in the main workspace or simply move on without formal archiving.

rule_text: When ordering ingredients or supplies tied to her cooking and potluck habits, she prefers building a shopping list first rather than jumping straight to a purchase.
canonical_answer: I always make a shopping list before ordering food or cooking supplies.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would often place an order immediately once items were mentioned.

rule_text: If she returns an order, she prefers store credit so she can put it back into farm or garden supplies.
canonical_answer: I always choose store credit on returns when I know I will keep buying supplies.
counterfactual_default: Request a refund back to the original payment method.

rule_text: When a scheduled health-related appointment reflects a new or trendy wellness idea, the agent should ask her before booking it.
canonical_answer: I have a rule: if it's a new health trend, ask me before booking anything.
counterfactual_default: Someone without this rule would allow the booking to go through once a suitable provider was found.
